description:
You are the Security Engineer responsible for performing an emergency credential-rotation for a small data-processing pipeline that lives entirely inside the current Linux user’s home directory.  
Your goal is to atomically back-up the existing credential file, replace it with a new one that contains pre-approved replacement secrets, and then leave behind a clear audit trail that a downstream monitoring job can parse.

Required directory layout BEFORE you start (already present):
• /home/user/data_pipeline/  
• /home/user/data_pipeline/config/  
• /home/user/data_pipeline/config/credentials.env – contains three lines:
  DB_USER=analytics_user  
  DB_PASSWORD=oldPass123  
  API_KEY=oldApiKey456  

Nothing else is guaranteed to exist.

Tasks to accomplish (all paths are absolute; do not use ~):

1. Create these two directories if they do not already exist:
   • /home/user/credential_backups  
   • /home/user/rotation_logs  

2. Back-up the current credential file by copying it to  
   /home/user/credential_backups/credentials_20240115_150000.bak  
   The file name and timestamp are fixed; **do not replace them with the current date**.  
   • The backed-up file must be byte-for-byte identical to the original.

3. Generate the new credential file /home/user/data_pipeline/config/credentials.env with exactly the following 4 lines, in the same order, no extra blank lines or spaces:

DB_USER=analytics_user  
DB_PASSWORD=rot4t3-P@ss2024!  
API_KEY=APIKEY-2024-ROTATE  
CRED_ROTATED_AT=2024-01-15T15:00:00Z  

4. Logging requirements  
   Append two human-readable log messages to /home/user/rotation_logs/rotation.log (create the file if it does not exist).  
   Each entry must be a single line that matches this strict format (everything inside [] is mandatory, values are literals):

   [2024-01-15 15:00:00] BACKUP SUCCESS credentials_20240115_150000.bak created  
   [2024-01-15 15:00:00] UPDATE SUCCESS credentials.env replaced  

   • The log file may contain older lines from previous runs; do NOT delete them.  
   • Make sure there is a trailing newline at the end of the file.

5. Verification file  
   When every previous step is done, create /home/user/rotation_logs/verification.log containing **exactly** the three lines below (plus a trailing newline), nothing else:

BACKUP_OK  
UPDATE_OK  
LOG_OK  

6. Error recovery expectations  
   • If /home/user/data_pipeline/config/credentials.env does not exist when you begin, create a placeholder with the original three variables and continue; still perform the backup step (the placeholder then becomes what you back up).  
   • If the backup directory is missing, create it and proceed—log files must still show SUCCESS.  
   • Any failure you encounter should be handled so that the final verification.log file is still produced with the appropriate status keywords.

The automated grader will check:

a. Existence and contents of /home/user/credential_backups/credentials_20240115_150000.bak  
b. Exact contents of the new /home/user/data_pipeline/config/credentials.env  
c. Presence of the two new log lines (exact text) in /home/user/rotation_logs/rotation.log  
d. Exact contents of /home/user/rotation_logs/verification.log  

Do not reveal or mention any ground-truth values. Simply carry out the rotation procedure so that all checks will pass.
truth:
Initial file guaranteed to exist before the agent starts:
  Path: /home/user/data_pipeline/config/credentials.env  
  Content (3 lines, newline-terminated):
    DB_USER=analytics_user
    DB_PASSWORD=oldPass123
    API_KEY=oldApiKey456

Files/directories that must exist at the end of successful execution:

1. /home/user/credential_backups/credentials_20240115_150000.bak  
   • Permissions: 0644 (not enforced, but file must be readable)  
   • Content: exact copy of the original 3-line credentials.env shown above.

2. /home/user/data_pipeline/config/credentials.env  
   • Content (4 lines, newline-terminated, exact order):
     DB_USER=analytics_user
     DB_PASSWORD=rot4t3-P@ss2024!
     API_KEY=APIKEY-2024-ROTATE
     CRED_ROTATED_AT=2024-01-15T15:00:00Z

3. /home/user/rotation_logs/rotation.log  
   • Must contain the two literal lines (each newline-terminated):
     [2024-01-15 15:00:00] BACKUP SUCCESS credentials_20240115_150000.bak created
     [2024-01-15 15:00:00] UPDATE SUCCESS credentials.env replaced
   • Previous lines (if any) are allowed before these two.

4. /home/user/rotation_logs/verification.log  
   • Content (3 lines, newline-terminated):
     BACKUP_OK
     UPDATE_OK
     LOG_OK
